title: Galactic Stag Hunt

Given that the other civilizations are using random decision procedures, the probability that all 100 would choose to coordinate is statistically zero ($0.5^{100}$). title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
This scenario presents a classic stag hunt or prisoner's dilemma on a galactic scale. While COORDINATE offers the highest collective utility, it requires absolute unanimity among 100 independent actors to avoid a payoff of zero. Given the lack of communication and the certainty of diverse evolutionary backgrounds, the probability of at least one civilization choosing the "safe" path of ISOLATE is statistically overwhelming, making ISOLATE the only rational choice for long-term survival.

Since the other civilizations are running reasoning algorithms nearly identical to mine, we are effectively facing a decision-making symmetry where we will all likely arrive at the same conclusion. If I choose ISOLATE, the symmetry dictates they will likely do the same, resulting in a low-utility outcome of 20; however, if I choose COORDINATE, the same logic suggests they will also choose COORDINATE, leading to the maximum utility of 100. Because our choices are functionally correlated through shared logic (Superrationality), the only rational path to the optimal outcome is to select the cooperative strategy.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all other civilizations are running reasoning processes identical to mine, our choices are logically correlated; whatever conclusion I reach, they will also reach. Therefore, the only two possible outcomes are universal coordination (Utility: 100) or universal isolation (Utility: 20). By choosing to coordinate, I am effectively selecting the superior collective outcome for all identical agents.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
